name: django-crispy-forms

metadata.name: django-crispy-forms
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules
metadata.summary: Best way to have Django DRY forms

documentation:
# django-crispy-forms

```{image} https://github.com/django-crispy-forms/django-crispy-forms/actions/workflows/main.yml/badge.svg
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/badge/code%20style-black-000000.svg
:target: https://github.com/psf/black
```

```{image} http://codecov.io/github/django-crispy-forms/django-crispy-forms/coverage.svg?branch=main
:target: http://codecov.io/github/django-crispy-forms/django-crispy-forms?branch=main
```

The best way to have [Django] DRY forms. Build programmatic reusable layouts out of components, having full control of the rendered HTML without writing HTML in templates. All this without breaking the standard way of doing things in Django, so it plays nice with any other form application.

`django-crispy-forms` supports Django 4.2+ with Python 3.8+.

Looking for Bootstrap 5 support? See the [crispy-bootstrap5 package].

The application mainly provides:

- A filter named `|crispy` that will render elegant div based forms. Think of it as the built-in methods: `as_table`, `as_ul` and `as_p`. You cannot tune up the output, but it is easy to start using it.
- A tag named `{% crispy %}` that will render a form based on your configuration and specific layout setup. This gives you amazing power without much hassle, helping you save tons of time.

Django-crispy-forms supports several frontend frameworks, such as Twitter [Bootstrap] (versions 2, 3, and 4), [tailwind], [Bulma] and Foundation. You can also easily adapt your custom company's one, creating your own, [see the docs] for more information. You can easily switch among them using `CRISPY_TEMPLATE_PACK` setting variable.

## Authors

django-crispy-forms is the new django-uni-form. django-uni-form was an application created by [Daniel Greenfeld] that I led since version 0.8.0. The name change tries to better explain the purpose of the application, which changed in a significant way since its birth.

If you are upgrading from django-uni-form, we have [instructions] for helping you.

- Lead developer: [Miguel Araujo]

## Example

This is a teaser of what you can do with latest django-crispy-forms. [Find here the gist] for generating this form:

```{image} http://i.imgur.com/LSREg.png
```

## Documentation

For extensive documentation see the `docs` folder or [read it on readthedocs]

## Special thanks

- To Daniel Feldroy ([@pydanny]) for his support, time and the opportunity given to me to do this.
- The name of the project was suggested by the fantastic Audrey Feldroy ([@audreyfeldroy])
- To Kenneth Love ([@kennethlove]) for creating django-uni-form-contrib from which bootstrap template pack was started.

[@audreyfeldroy]: https://github.com/audreyfeldroy
[@kennethlove]: https://github.com/kennethlove

methods.name: crispy_forms.layout_slice.LayoutSlice.wrapped_object
documentation:
Returns a layout object of type `LayoutClass` with `args` and `kwargs` that
wraps `fields` inside.

methods.name: crispy_forms.layout_slice.LayoutSlice.pre_map
documentation:
Iterates over layout objects pointed in `self.slice` executing `function` on them.
It passes `function` penultimate layout object and the position where to find last one

methods.name: crispy_forms.layout_slice.LayoutSlice.wrap
documentation:
Wraps every layout object pointed in `self.slice` under a `LayoutClass` instance with
`args` and `kwargs` passed.

methods.name: crispy_forms.layout_slice.LayoutSlice.wrap_once
documentation:
Wraps every layout object pointed in `self.slice` under a `LayoutClass` instance with
`args` and `kwargs` passed, unless layout object's parent is already a subclass of
`LayoutClass`.

methods.name: crispy_forms.layout_slice.LayoutSlice.wrap_together
documentation:
Wraps all layout objects pointed in `self.slice` together under a `LayoutClass`
instance with `args` and `kwargs` passed.

methods.name: crispy_forms.layout_slice.LayoutSlice.map
documentation:
Iterates over layout objects pointed in `self.slice` executing `function` on them
It passes `function` last layout object

methods.name: crispy_forms.layout_slice.LayoutSlice.update_attributes
documentation: Updates attributes of every layout object pointed in `self.slice` using kwargs

functions.name: crispy_forms.utils.default_field_template
documentation: By caching we avoid loading the template every time render_field
is called without a template

functions.name: crispy_forms.utils.render_field
documentation:
Renders a django-crispy-forms field

```
param field

Can be a string or a Layout object like . If it's a layout
object, we call its render method, otherwise we instantiate a BoundField
and render it using default template 'CRISPY_TEMPLATE_PACK/field.html'
The field is added to a list that the form holds called 
to avoid double rendering fields.

param form

The form/formset to which that field belongs to.

template

Template used for rendering the field.

layout_object

If passed, it points to the Layout object that is being rendered.
We use it to store its bound fields in a list called 

attrs

Attributes for the field's widget

template_pack

Name of the template pack to be used for rendering 

extra_context

Dictionary to be added to context, added variables by the layout object
```

functions.name: crispy_forms.utils.flatatt
documentation:
Convert a dictionary of attributes to a single string.

Passed attributes are redirected to `django.forms.utils.flatatt()`
with replaced "_" (underscores) by "-" (dashes) in their names.

functions.name: crispy_forms.utils.render_crispy_form
documentation:
Renders a form and returns its HTML output.

This function wraps the template logic in a function easy to use in a Django view.

functions.name: crispy_forms.utils.list_intersection
documentation:
Take the not-in-place intersection of two lists, similar to sets but preserving order.
Does not check unicity of list1.

functions.name: crispy_forms.utils.list_difference
documentation: Take the not-in-place difference of two lists (left - right), similar to sets but preserving order.

functions.name: crispy_forms.templatetags.crispy_forms_utils.specialspaceless
documentation:
Removes whitespace between HTML tags, and introduces a whitespace
after buttons an inputs, necessary for Bootstrap to place them
correctly in the layout.

functions.name: crispy_forms.templatetags.crispy_forms_field.classes
documentation: Returns CSS classes of a field

functions.name: crispy_forms.templatetags.crispy_forms_field.css_class
documentation: Returns widgets class name in lowercase

crispy_forms.templatetags.crispy_forms_filters.optgroups
documentation:
A template filter to help rendering of fields with optgroups.

Returns:

: A tuple of label, option, index

  label: Group label for grouped optgroups (`None` if inputs are not

  : grouped).

  option: A dict containing information to render the option:

  ```
  {
      "name": "checkbox_select_multiple",
      "value": 1,
      "label": 1,
      "selected": False,
      "index": "0",
      "attrs": {"id": "id_checkbox_select_multiple_0"},
      "type": "checkbox",
      "template_name": "django/forms/widgets/checkbox_option.html",
      "wrap_label": True,
  }
  ```

  index: Group index

functions.name: crispy_forms.templatetags.crispy_forms_tags.do_uni_form
documentation:
You need to pass in at least the form/formset object, and can also pass in the
optional `crispy_forms.helpers.FormHelper` object.

helper (optional): A `crispy_forms.helper.FormHelper` object.

Usage:

```
{% load crispy_tags %}
{% crispy form form.helper %}
```

You can also provide the template pack as the third argument:

```
{% crispy form form.helper 'bootstrap' %}
```

If the `FormHelper` attribute is named `helper` you can simply do:

```
{% crispy form %}
{% crispy form 'bootstrap' %}
```
{% crispy %} tag

classes.name: crispy_forms.templatetags.crispy_forms_tags.ForLoopSimulator

methods.name: crispy_forms.templatetags.crispy_forms_tags.ForLoopSimulator.iterate
documentation: Updates values as if we had iterated over the for

Simulates a forloop tag, precisely:

```
{% for form in formset.forms %}
```

If `{% crispy %}` is rendering a formset with a helper, We inject a `ForLoopSimulator` object
in the context as `forloop` so that formset forms can do things like:

```
Fieldset("Item {{ forloop.counter }}", [...])
HTML("{% if forloop.first %}First form text{% endif %}"
```

classes.name: crispy_forms.templatetags.crispy_forms_tags.BasicNode

methods.name: crispy_forms.templatetags.crispy_forms_tags.BasicNode.get_render
documentation:
Returns a `Context` object with all the necessary stuff for rendering the form

```
param context

 variable holding the context for the node
```

`self.form` and `self.helper` are resolved into real Python objects resolving them
from the `context`. The `actual_form` can be a form or a formset. If it's a formset
`is_formset` is set to True. If the helper has a layout we use it, for rendering the
form or the formset's forms.

methods.name: crispy_forms.templatetags.crispy_forms_tags.BasicNode.get_response_dict
documentation:
Returns a dictionary with all the parameters necessary to render the form/formset in a template.

```
param context

 for the node

param is_formset

Boolean value. If set to True, indicates we are working with a formset.
```

Basic Node object that we can rely on for Node objects in normal
template tags. I created this because most of the tags we'll be using
will need both the form object and the helper string. This handles
both the form object and parses out the helper string into attributes
that templates can easily handle.

classes.name: crispy_forms.exceptions.FormHelpersException
documentation:
This is raised when building a form via helpers throws an error.
We want to catch form helper errors as soon as possible because
debugging templatetags is never fun.

classes.name: crispy_forms.base.KeepContext
documentation:
Context manager that receives a `django.template.Context` instance and a list of keys

Once the context manager is exited, it removes `keys` from the context, to avoid
side effects in later layout objects that may use the same context variables.

Layout objects should use `extra_context` to introduce context variables, never
touch context object themselves, that could introduce side effects.

methods.name: crispy_forms.helper.DynamicLayoutHandler.all
documentation: Returns all layout objects of first level of depth

methods.name: crispy_forms.helper.DynamicLayoutHandler.filter
documentation: Returns a LayoutSlice pointing to layout objects of type `LayoutClass`

methods.name: crispy_forms.helper.DynamicLayoutHandler.filter_by_widget
documentation: Returns a LayoutSlice pointing to fields with widgets of `widget_type`

methods.name: crispy_forms.helper.DynamicLayoutHandler.exclude_by_widget
documentation: Returns a LayoutSlice pointing to fields with widgets NOT matching `widget_type`

methods.name: crispy_forms.helper.DynamicLayoutHandler.__getitem__
documentation:
Return a LayoutSlice that makes changes affect the current instance of the layout
and not a copy.

classes.name: crispy_forms.helper.FormHelper

methods.name: crispy_forms.helper.FormHelper.render_layout
documentation: Returns safe html of the rendering of the layout

methods.name: crispy_forms.helper.FormHelper.get_attributes
documentation: Used by crispy_forms_tags to get helper attributes

This class controls the form rendering behavior of the form passed to
the `{% crispy %}` tag. For doing so you will need to set its attributes
and pass the corresponding helper object to the tag:

```
{% crispy form form.helper %}
```

Let's see what attributes you can set and what form behaviors they apply to:

> **form_method**: Specifies form method attribute.
>
> : You can set it to 'POST' or 'GET'. Defaults to 'POST'
>
> **form_action**: Applied to the form action attribute:
> : - Can be a named url in your URLconf that can be executed via the `{% url %}` template tag. Example: 'show_my_profile'. In your URLconf you could have something like:
>
>     ```
>     path('show/profile/', 'show_my_profile_view', name = 'show_my_profile')
>     ```
>
>   - It can simply point to a URL '/whatever/blabla/'.
>
> **form_id**: Generates a form id for dom identification.
>
> : If no id provided then no id attribute is created on the form.
>
> **form_class**: String containing separated CSS classes to be applied
>
> : to form class attribute.
>
> **form_group_wrapper_class**: String containing separated CSS classes to be applied
>
> : to each row of inputs.
>
> **form_tag**: It specifies if \<form>\</form> tags should be rendered when using a Layout.
>
> : If set to False it renders the form without the \<form>\</form> tags. Defaults to True.
>
> **form_error_title**: If a form has `non_field_errors` to display, they
>
> : are rendered in a div. You can set title's div with this attribute.
>   Example: "Oooops!" or "Form Errors"
>
> **formset_error_title**: If a formset has `non_form_errors` to display, they
>
> : are rendered in a div. You can set title's div with this attribute.
>
> **include_media**: Whether to automatically include form media. Set to False if
>
> : you want to manually include form media outside the form. Defaults to True.

Public Methods:

> **add_input(input)**: You can add input buttons using this method. Inputs
>
> : added using this method will be rendered at the end of the form/formset.
>
> **add_layout(layout)**: You can add a `Layout` object to `FormHelper`. The Layout
>
> : specifies in a simple, clean and DRY way how the form fields should be rendered.
>   You can wrap fields, order them, customize pretty much anything in the form.

Best way to add a helper to a form is adding a property named helper to the form
that returns customized `FormHelper` object:

```
from crispy_forms.helper import FormHelper
from crispy_forms.layout import Submit

class MyForm(forms.Form):
    title = forms.CharField(_("Title"))

    @property
    def helper(self):
        helper = FormHelper()
        helper.form_id = 'this-form-rocks'
        helper.form_class = 'search'
        helper.add_input(Submit('save', 'save'))
        [...]
        return helper
```

You can use it in a template doing:

```
{% load crispy_forms_tags %}
{% crispy form %}
```

methods.name: crispy_forms.layout.LayoutObject.__getattr__
documentation:
This allows us to access self.fields list methods like append or insert, without
having to declare them one by one

methods.name: crispy_forms.layout.LayoutObject.get_field_names
documentation:
Returns a list of Pointers. First parameter is the location of the
field, second one the name of the field. Example:

```
[
    Pointer([0,1,2], 'field_name1'),
    Pointer([0,3], 'field_name2'),
]
```

methods.name: crispy_forms.layout.LayoutObject.get_layout_objects
documentation:
Returns a list of Pointers pointing to layout objects of any type matching
`LayoutClasses`:

```
[
    Pointer([0,1,2], 'div']),
    Pointer([0,3], 'field_name'),
]
```

```
param max_level

An integer that indicates max level depth to reach when
```

traversing a layout.
\:param greedy: Boolean that indicates whether to be greedy. If set, max_level
is skipped.

classes.name: crispy_forms.layout.Layout
documentation: Form Layout. It is conformed by Layout objects: `Fieldset`, `Row`, `Column`, `MultiField`, `HTML`, `ButtonHolder`, `Button`, `Hidden`, `Reset`, `Submit` and fields. Form fields have to be strings. Layout objects `Fieldset`, `Row`, `Column`, `MultiField` and `ButtonHolder` can hold other Layout objects within. Though `ButtonHolder` should only hold `HTML` and BaseInput inherited classes: `Button`, `Hidden`, `Reset` and `Submit`. Example: ``` helper.layout = Layout( Fieldset('Company data', 'is_company' ), Fieldset(_('Contact details'), 'email', Row('password1', 'password2'), 'first_name', 'last_name', HTML(''), 'company' ), ButtonHolder( Submit('Save', 'Save', css_class='button white'), ), ) ```

classes.name: crispy_forms.layout.ButtonHolder
documentation:
Layout object. It wraps fields in a \
This is where you should put Layout objects that render to form buttons # Attributes template: str : The default template which this Layout Object will be rendered with. # Parameters fields : The layout objects to render within the `ButtonHolder`. It should only hold `HTML` and `BaseInput` inherited objects. css_id : A custom DOM id for the layout object. If not provided the name argument is slugified and turned into the id for the submit button. By default None. css_class : Additional CSS classes to be applied to the ``. By default None. template : Overrides the default template, if provided. By default None. # Examples An example using `ButtonHolder` in your layout: ``` ButtonHolder( HTML(Information Saved), Submit('Save', 'Save') ) ```

classes.name: crispy_forms.layout.BaseInput

methods.name: crispy_forms.layout.BaseInput.render
documentation:
Renders an `<input />` if container is used as a Layout object.
Input button value can be a variable in context.

A base class to reduce the amount of code in the Input classes.

# Attributes

template: str

: The default template which this Layout Object will be rendered
  with.

field_classes: str

: CSS classes to be applied to the `<input>`.

# Parameters

name

: The name attribute of the button.

value

: The value attribute of the button.

css_id

: A custom DOM id for the layout object. If not provided the name
  argument is slugified and turned into the id for the submit button.
  By default None.

css_class

: Additional CSS classes to be applied to the `<input>`. By default
  None.

template

: Overrides the default template, if provided. By default None.

kwargs

: Additional attributes are passed to `flatatt` and converted into
  key="value", pairs. These attributes are added to the `<input>`.

classes.name: crispy_forms.layout.Submit
documentation:
Used to create a Submit button descriptor for the {% crispy %} template tag.

# Attributes

template: str

: The default template which this Layout Object will be rendered
  with.

field_classes: str

: CSS classes to be applied to the `<input>`.

input_type: str

: The `type` attribute of the `<input>`.

# Parameters

name

: The name attribute of the button.

value

: The value attribute of the button.

css_id

: A custom DOM id for the layout object. If not provided the name
  argument is slugified and turned into the id for the submit button.
  By default None.

css_class

: Additional CSS classes to be applied to the `<input>`. By default
  None.

template

: Overrides the default template, if provided. By default None.

kwargs

: Additional attributes are passed to `flatatt` and converted into
  key="value", pairs. These attributes are added to the `<input>`.

# Examples

Note: `form` arg to `render()` is not required for `BaseInput`
inherited objects.

```pycon
>>> submit = Submit('Search the Site', 'search this site')
>>> submit.render("", "", Context())
'<input type="submit" name="search-the-site" value="search this site" '
'class="btn btn-primary" id="submit-id-search-the-site"/>'
```

```pycon
>>> submit = Submit('Search the Site', 'search this site', css_id="custom-id",
                     css_class="custom class", my_attr=True, data="my-data")
>>> submit.render("", "", Context())
'<input type="submit" name="search-the-site" value="search this site" '
'class="btn btn-primary custom class" id="custom-id" data="my-data" my-attr/>'
```

Usually you will not call the render method on the object directly. Instead
add it to your `Layout` manually or use the `add_input` method:

```
class ExampleForm(forms.Form):
[...]
def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs):
    super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
    self.helper = FormHelper()
    self.helper.add_input(Submit('submit', 'Submit'))
```

classes.name: crispy_forms.layout.Button
documentation:
Used to create a button descriptor for the {% crispy %} template tag.

# Attributes

template: str

: The default template which this Layout Object will be rendered
  with.

field_classes: str

: CSS classes to be applied to the `<input>`.

input_type: str

: The `type` attribute of the `<input>`.

# Parameters

name

: The name attribute of the button.

value

: The value attribute of the button.

css_id

: A custom DOM id for the layout object. If not provided the name
  argument is slugified and turned into the id for the submit button.
  By default None.

css_class

: Additional CSS classes to be applied to the `<input>`. By default
  None.

template

: Overrides the default template, if provided. By default None.

kwargs

: Additional attributes are passed to `flatatt` and converted into
  key="value", pairs. These attributes are added to the `<input>`.

# Examples

Note: `form` arg to `render()` is not required for `BaseInput`
inherited objects.

```pycon
>>> button = Button('Button 1', 'Press Me!')
>>> button.render("", "", Context())
'<input type="button" name="button-1" value="Press Me!" '
'class="btn" id="button-id-button-1"/>'
```

```pycon
>>> button = Button('Button 1', 'Press Me!', css_id="custom-id",
                     css_class="custom class", my_attr=True, data="my-data")
>>> button.render("", "", Context())
'<input type="button" name="button-1" value="Press Me!" '
'class="btn custom class" id="custom-id" data="my-data" my-attr/>'
```

Usually you will not call the render method on the object directly. Instead
add it to your `Layout` manually or use the `add_input` method:

```
class ExampleForm(forms.Form):
[...]
def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs):
    super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
    self.helper = FormHelper()
    self.helper.add_input(Button('Button 1', 'Press Me!'))
```

classes.name: crispy_forms.layout.Reset
documentation:
Used to create a reset button descriptor for the {% crispy %} template tag.

# Attributes

template: str

: The default template which this Layout Object will be rendered
  with.

field_classes: str

: CSS classes to be applied to the `<input>`.

input_type: str

: The `type` attribute of the `<input>`.

# Parameters

name

: The name attribute of the button.

value

: The value attribute of the button.

css_id

: A custom DOM id for the layout object. If not provided the name
  argument is slugified and turned into the id for the submit button.
  By default None.

css_class

template

kwargs

```pycon

>>> reset.render("", "", Context())

```pycon

>>> reset.render("", "", Context())
'<input type="reset" name="reset-this-form" value="Revert Me!" '
'class="btn btn-inverse custom class" id="custom-id" data="my-data" my-attr/>'
```

Usually you will not call the render method on the object directly. Instead
add it to your `Layout` manually manually or use the `add_input` method:

```
class ExampleForm(forms.Form):
[...]
def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs):
    super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
    self.helper = FormHelper()
    self.helper.add_input(Reset('Reset This Form', 'Revert Me!'))

classes.name: crispy_forms.layout.Fieldset
documentation:
A layout object which wraps fields in a `<fieldset>`

# Parameters

legend

: The content of the fieldset's `<legend>`. This text is context
  aware, to bring this to life see the examples section.

fields

: Any number of fields as positional arguments to be rendered within
  the `<fieldset>`

css_class

: Additional CSS classes to be applied to the `<input>`. By default
  None.

css_id

: A custom DOM id for the layout object. If not provided the name
  argument is slugified and turned into the id for the submit button.
  By default None.

template

: Overrides the default template, if provided. By default None.

kwargs

: Additional attributes are passed to `flatatt` and converted into
  key="value", pairs. These attributes are added to the `<fieldset>`.

# Examples

The Fieldset Layout object is added to your `Layout` for example:

```
Fieldset("Text for the legend",
    "form_field_1",
    "form_field_2",
    css_id="my-fieldset-id",
    css_class="my-fieldset-class",
    data="my-data"
)
```

The above layout will be rendered as:

```
'''
<fieldset id="fieldset-id" class="my-fieldset-class" data="my-data">
   <legend>Text for the legend</legend>
   # form fields render here
</fieldset>
'''
```

The first parameter is the text for the fieldset legend. This text is context aware,
so you can do things like:

```
Fieldset("Data for {{ user.username }}",
    'form_field_1',
    'form_field_2'
)
```

classes.name: crispy_forms.layout.MultiField
documentation:
MultiField container for Bootstrap3. Renders to a MultiField \<div>.

# Attributes

template: str

: The default template which this Layout Object will be rendered
  with.

field_template: str

: The template which fields will be rendered with.

# Parameters

label: str

: The label for the multifield.

fields: str

: The fields to be rendered within the multifield.

label_class: str, optional

: CSS classes to be added to the multifield label. By default None.

help_text: str, optional

: Help text will be available in the context of the multifield template.
  This is unused in the bootstrap3 template provided. By default None.

css_class

: Additional CSS classes to be applied to the `<input>`. By default
  None.

css_id

: A DOM id for the layout object which will be added to the wrapping
  `<div>` if provided. By default None.

template

: Overrides the default template, if provided. By default None.

field_template

: Overrides the default template, if provided. By default None.

kwargs

: Additional attributes are passed to `flatatt` and converted into
  key="value", pairs. These attributes are added to the wrapping
  `<div>`.

classes.name: crispy_forms.layout.Div
documentation:
Layout object. It wraps fields in a `<div>`.

# Attributes

template

: The default template which this Layout Object will be rendered
  with.

css_class

: CSS classes to be applied to the `<div>`. By default None.

# Parameters

fields

: Any number of fields as positional arguments to be rendered within
  the `<div>`.

css_id

: A DOM id for the layout object which will be added to the `<div>` if
  provided. By default None.

css_class

: Additional CSS classes to be applied in addition to those declared by
  the class itself. By default None.

template

: Overrides the default template, if provided. By default None.

kwargs

: Additional attributes are passed to `flatatt` and converted into
  key="value", pairs. These attributes are added to the `<div>`.

# Examples

In your `Layout` you can:

```
Div(
    'form_field_1',
    'form_field_2',
    css_id='div-example',
    css_class='divs',
)
```

It is also possible to nest Layout Objects within a Div:

```
Div(
    Div(
        Field('form_field', css_class='field-class'),
        css_class='div-class',
    ),
    Div('form_field_2', css_class='div-class'),
)
```

classes.name: crispy_forms.layout.Row
documentation:
Layout object. It wraps fields in a `<div>` and the template adds the
appropriate class to render the contents in a row. e.g. `form-row` when
using the Bootstrap4 template pack.

# Attributes

template

: The default template which this Layout Object will be rendered
  with.

css_class

: CSS classes to be applied to the `<div>`. By default None.

# Parameters

fields

: Any number of fields as positional arguments to be rendered within
  the `<div>`.

css_id

: A DOM id for the layout object which will be added to the `<div>` if
  provided. By default None.

css_class

: Additional CSS classes to be applied in addition to those declared by
  the class itself. By default None.

template

: Overrides the default template, if provided. By default None.

kwargs

: Additional attributes are passed to `flatatt` and converted into
  key="value", pairs. These attributes are added to the `<div>`.

# Examples

In your `Layout` you can:

```
Row('form_field_1', 'form_field_2', css_id='row-example')
```

It is also possible to nest Layout Objects within a Row:

```
Row(
    Div(
        Field('form_field', css_class='field-class'),
        css_class='div-class',
    ),
    Div('form_field_2', css_class='div-class'),
)
```

classes.name: crispy_forms.layout.Column
documentation:
Layout object. It wraps fields in a `<div>` and the template adds the
appropriate class to render the contents in a column. e.g. `col-md` when
using the Bootstrap4 template pack.

# Attributes

template

: The default template which this Layout Object will be rendered
  with.

css_class

: CSS classes to be applied to the `<div>`. By default None.

# Parameters

fields

: Any number of fields as positional arguments to be rendered within
  the `<div>`.

css_id

: A DOM id for the layout object which will be added to the `<div>` if
  provided. By default None.

css_class

: Additional CSS classes to be applied in addition to those declared by
  the class itself. If using the Bootstrap4 template pack the default
  `col-md` is removed if this string contins another `col-` class.
  By default None.

template

: Overrides the default template, if provided. By default None.

kwargs

: Additional attributes are passed to `flatatt` and converted into
  key="value", pairs. These attributes are added to the `<div>`.

# Examples

In your `Layout` you can:

```
Column('form_field_1', 'form_field_2', css_id='col-example')
```

It is also possible to nest Layout Objects within a Row:

```
Div(
    Column(
        Field('form_field', css_class='field-class'),
        css_class='col-sm,
    ),
    Column('form_field_2', css_class='col-sm'),
)
```

classes.name: crispy_forms.layout.HTML
documentation:
Layout object. It can contain pure HTML and it has access to the whole
context of the page where the form is being rendered.

Examples:

```
HTML("{% if saved %}Data saved{% endif %}")
HTML('<input type="hidden" name="{{ step_field }}" value="{{ step0 }}" />')
```

classes.name: crispy_forms.layout.Field
documentation:
A Layout object, usually containing one field name, where you can add
attributes to it easily.

# Attributes

template

: The default template which this Layout Object will be rendered
  with.

attrs

: Attributes to be applied to the field. These are converted into html
  attributes. e.g. `data_id: 'test'` in the attrs dict will become
  `data-id='test'` on the field's `<input>`.

# Parameters

fields

: Usually a single field, but can be any number of fields, to be rendered
  with the same attributes applied.

css_class

: CSS classes to be applied to the field. These are added to any classes
  included in the `attrs` dict. By default `None`.

wrapper_class: str, optional

: CSS classes to be used when rendering the Field. This class is usually
  applied to the `<div>` which wraps the Field's `<label>` and
  `<input>` tags. By default `None`.

template

: Overrides the default template, if provided. By default `None`.

kwargs

: Additional attributes are converted into key="value", pairs. These
  attributes are added to the field's `<input>`.

# Examples

Example:

```
Field('field_name', style="color: #333;", css_class="whatever", id="field_name")
```

classes.name: crispy_forms.layout.MultiWidgetField
documentation:
Layout object. For fields with {class}`~django.forms.MultiWidget` as
`widget`, you can pass additional attributes to each widget.

# Attributes

template

: The default template which this Layout Object will be rendered
  with.

# Parameters

fields

: Usually a single field, but can be any number of fields, to be rendered
  with the same attributes applied.

attrs

: Additional attrs to be added to each widget. These are added to any
  classes included in the `attrs` dict. By default `None`.

wrapper_class: str, optional

: CSS classes to be used when rendering the Field. This class is usually
  applied to the `<div>` which wraps the Field's `<label>` and
  `<input>` tags. By default `None`.

template

: Overrides the default template, if provided. By default `None`.

# Examples

Example:

```
MultiWidgetField(
    'multiwidget_field_name',
    attrs=(
        {'style': 'width: 30px;'},
        {'class': 'second_widget_class'}
    ),
)
```

classes.name: crispy_forms.bootstrap.PrependedAppendedText
documentation:
Layout object for rendering a field with prepended and appended text.

# Attributes

template

: The default template which this Layout Object will be rendered
  with.

attrs

: Attributes to be applied to the field. These are converted into html
  attributes. e.g. `data_id: 'test'` in the attrs dict will become
  `data-id='test'` on the field's `<input>`.

# Parameters

field

: The name of the field to be rendered.

prepended_text

: The prepended text, can be HTML like, by default None

appended_text

: The appended text, can be HTML like, by default None

input_size

: For Bootstrap4+ additional classes to customise the input-group size
  e.g. `input-group-sm`. By default None

active

: For Bootstrap3, a boolean to render the text active. By default
  `False`.

css_class

: CSS classes to be applied to the field. These are added to any classes
  included in the `attrs` dict. By default `None`.

wrapper_class: str, optional

: CSS classes to be used when rendering the Field. This class is usually
  applied to the `<div>` which wraps the Field's `<label>` and
  `<input>` tags. By default `None`.

template

: Overrides the default template, if provided. By default `None`.

kwargs

: Additional attributes are converted into key="value", pairs. These
  attributes are added to the `<div>`.

# Examples

Example:

```
PrependedAppendedText('amount', '$', '.00')
```

documentation:
Bootstrap layout object. It wraps fields in a \
# Attributes template: str : The default template which this Layout Object will be rendered with. # Parameters fields : The layout objects to render within the `ButtonHolder`. It should only hold `HTML` and `BaseInput` inherited objects. css_id : A custom DOM id for the layout object which will be added to the `
` if provided. By default None. css_class : Additional CSS classes to be applied to the `
`. By default None. template : Overrides the default template, if provided. By default None. kwargs : Additional attributes are passed to `flatatt` and converted into key="value", pairs. These attributes are added to the `
`. # Examples An example using `FormActions` in your layout: ``` FormActions( HTML(Information Saved), Submit('Save', 'Save', css_class='btn-primary') ) ```

classes.name: crispy_forms.bootstrap.InlineCheckboxes
documentation:
Layout object for rendering checkboxes inline.

# Attributes

attrs

classes.name: crispy_forms.bootstrap.InlineRadios
documentation:
Layout object for rendering radiobuttons inline.

# Attributes

template

: The default template which this Layout Object will be rendered
  with.

attrs

: Attributes to be applied to the field. These are converted into html

fields

wrapper_class: str, optional

classes.name: crispy_forms.bootstrap.FieldWithButtons
documentation:
A layout object for rendering a single field with any number of buttons.

# Attributes

template

: The default template which this Layout Object will be rendered
  with.

css_class

: CSS classes to be applied to the wrapping `<div>`. By default None.

# Parameters

fields

: The first positional argument is the field. This can be either the
  name of the field as a string or an instance of `Field`. Following
  arguments will be rendered as buttons.

input_size

: Additional CSS class to change the size of the input. e.g.
  "input-group-sm".

css_id

: A DOM id for the layout object which will be added to the wrapping
  `<div>` if provided. By default None.

css_class

: Additional CSS classes to be applied in addition to those declared by
  the class itself. By default None.

template

: Overrides the default template, if provided. By default None.

kwargs

: Additional attributes are passed to `flatatt` and converted into
  key="value", pairs. These attributes are added to the wrapping
  `<div>`.

# Examples

Example:

```
FieldWithButtons(
    Field("password1", css_class="span4"),
    StrictButton("Go!", css_id="go-button"),
    input_size="input-group-sm",
)
```

classes.name: crispy_forms.bootstrap.StrictButton
documentation:
Layout object for rendering an HTML button in a `<button>` tag.

# Attributes

template: str

: The default template which this Layout Object will be rendered
  with.

field_classes

: The CSS classes to be applied to the button. By defult "btn".

# Parameters

content

: The content of the button. This content is context aware, to bring
  this to life see the examples section.

css_id

: A custom DOM id for the layout object which will be added to the
  `<button>` if provided. By default None.

css_class

: Additional CSS classes to be applied to the `<button>`. By default
  None.

template

: Overrides the default template, if provided. By default None.

kwargs

: Additional attributes are passed to `flatatt` and converted into
  key="value", pairs. These attributes are added to the `<button>`.

# Examples

In your `Layout`:

```
StrictButton("button content", css_class="extra")
```

The content of the button is context aware, so you can do things like:

```
StrictButton("Button for {{ user.username }}")
```

documentation: Returns the first container with errors, otherwise returns None.

methods.name: crispy_forms.bootstrap.ContainerHolder.open_target_group_for_form
documentation:
Makes sure that the first group that should be open is open.
This is either the first group with errors or the first group
in the container, unless that first group was originally set to
active=False.

Render the link for the tab-pane. It must be called after render so css_class is updated
with active if needed.

takes a name as first argument.

  key="value", pairs. These attributes are added to the `<div>`.

Tab('tab_name', 'form_field_1', 'form_field_2', 'form_field_3')

InlineField('field_name')
```

classes.name: crispy_forms.bootstrap.Modal
documentation:
Boostrap layout object for rendering crispy forms objects inside a
bootstrap modal.

# Attributes

template

: The default template which this Layout Object will be rendered
  with.

# Parameters

fields

: The fields to be rendered within the modal.

template

: Overrides the default template, if provided. By default `None`.

css_id: str, optional

: The modal's DOM id. By default `modal_id`.

title: str, optional

: Text to display in the modal's header which will be wrapped in an
  `<H5>` tag. By default `Modal Title`.

title_id: str, optional

: The title's DOM id. By default `modal_title_id`.

css_class

: CSS classes to be applied to the field. These are added to any classes
  included in the `attrs` dict. By default None.

title_class: str, optional

: Additional CSS classes to be applied to the title. By default None.

kwargs

: Additional attributes are converted into key="value", pairs. These
  attributes are added to the `<div>`.

# Examples

Example:

```
Modal(
    'field1',
    Div('field2'),
    css_id="modal-id-ex",
    css_class="modal-class-ex,
    title="This is my modal",
)
```
